posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 557 others Pawlak vs. KhalidovMamed Khalidov, TKO, R1In no world can I reasonably back a 46 year old, but Mamed somehow defies age. He is a living legend. We saw last fight that Bartos is kin of fraudulent, but Mamed being the one to take his 0 was crazy. I thought Mamed was done for after the Robocop KO but he’s somehow kept winning. His skills are pretty insane and he’s also juiced to the gills, giving him about 10 more years of youth. His durability hasn’t cracked yet which is the main worry for guys over 40, and he won’t have to worry here as Pawel is not a good finisher. His 7-0 ksw run is impressive but his wins really aren’t that great. He couldn’t chin the ghost of Materla (one of the worst chins in the sport) and the only finishes on his run are 2 r5 finishes. A lot of his wins are against one dimensional guys or washed cans. Mamed might be too old at 46 but again, he defies any sort of normal logicDe Fries vs. WójcikMarcin Wójcik, TKO, R1PDF is probably gonna stay undefeated in ksw until he decides to call it, but this is probably the toughest test he’s had on this run. Wojcik has been running through guys since moving to 205 and has almost as much experience as PDF. He has good tdd which is key to beat PDF, an Defintely the better striker. Expecting a PDF domination but his time is coming soonVrtačić vs. OdzimkowskiAndi Vrtačić, TKO, R2This is gonna be a very violent fight; Vrtacic fights like a psycho. He’s tough to back because he has no defense and gets dropped in every fight, but he also loves a war and has brutal ko power. Every fight he gets sat on his ass bad and then makes a crazy comeback. It’s hard to back him against decent guys but Albert is old and been finished a bunch himself. This should’ve a slugfestRutkowski vs. PolischuckOleksiy Polischuck, DECVery unsure where to go here. Polishchuk is super talented but his tdd is awful. Hes got an active guard but makes no attempts to get to his feet. H

posts.author: Josh7
posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 557 others Pawlak vs. KhalidovPaweł Pawlak, TKO, R3Pawlak by TKO. Age advantage for him. Khalidov is almost 46 years old, does he has the stamina for this fight?De Fries vs. WójcikPhil De Fries, TKO, R2Reach advantage for De Fries. Win by TKO.Rutkowski vs. PolischuckDaniel Rutkowski, DECRutkowski by decision regardless of the age disadvantage. Most likely, he has great fight iQ.Vrtačić vs. OdzimkowskiAlbert Odzimkowski, TKO, R1Massive age disadvantage for Odzimkowski. Multiple TKO against strong opponent I will take my chances with Odzimkowski, he's on 4-win streak by TKO at 38 years old, wow!Paiva vs. DeuxWelisson Paiva, DECPaiva by decision. He has won against many fighters with strong record. Massive age advantage as well for him.Le vs. DecowskiSebastian Decowski, TKO, R2Decowski by TKO. Undefeated.Belhouachi vs. MašekJan Mašek, DECMašek by decision but this can go either way.

posts.author: Mazeflare
posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 557 others Not hating on Big Phil but if he wins this, might as well close the division, my understanding is that he does very little for KSW promotion-wise, low-profile and the fight style does help either.Or start being creative with the matchmaking, bring some young kickboxers or boxers (they always find one out of nowhere) and go for broke.
